title: Collecting eDNA from Marine Water Samples in the Field
author: Reindert Nijland
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.6yfhftn
materials: The DNA is now collected on the filter. Either directly continue with the DNA isolation protocol of choice, or store the preserved the DNA on the filter, depending on preservation method used. Qiagen DNeasy kit, short term (<48h) preservation: Store filter in 400 µL, ATL buffer from the DNeasy kit, shake well, store at Room temperature or below. Zymo DNA/RNA shield, short+longterm preservation: Store filter in 400 µL, DNA/RNA Shield , shake well, store at Room temperature or below. Longmire’s solution: longterm preservation: Store filter in 500 µL - 1 mL, longmire’s solution , shake well, store at RT or below cheap, works ok, but only compatible with Phenol/Chlorophorm extraction, which is laborious and also does not yield the best and purest DNA. Other alternative preservation methods : ethanol, freezing, DMSO, DESS, dehydration, combinations, etc.
In the lab, add 20 µL, ProtK .
Vigorously vortex the tube with filter+longmire solution.

steps.title: Collect water in an appropriate sterile, DNA free container
steps.instruction: Collect water in an appropriate sterile, DNA free container. For marine samples collected during scuba diving, we use a small hand-pump and a punch-balloon.

steps.title: Filter 1 L - 2 L...
steps.instruction: Filter 1 L - 2 L with the 250 ml filters (0.8 micron, cellulose nitrate) from Nalgene.

steps.title: Connect filter unit to vacuum pump, filter by vacuum
steps.instruction: Connect filter unit to vacuum pump, filter by vacuum. I use a small vacuum-pump from ebay, powered with a 12V power pack. (DC12V Mini Vacuum Pump Negative Pressure Suction Pump)

steps.title: Using the vacuum, make sure the...
steps.instruction: Using the vacuum, make sure the filter is dry, as to much transfer of liquid influences preservation and downstream DNA isolation.

steps.title: Cut filter from filter unit, it is glued in place
steps.instruction: Cut filter from filter unit, it is glued in place. No problem to cut off outer 0.5 cm of filter. Use sterile scalpel + scalpel holder. Fold filter twice using scalpel+tweezers.

steps.title: Insert filter in 2 ml screwcap...
steps.instruction: Insert filter in 2 ml screwcap tube prefilled with 400 µL, preservation solution . It is possible to use other tubes, but not when using 3d-fuge, as these do not fit and will snap open during centrifugation.

steps.title: Shake tube with filter, to fully...
steps.instruction: Shake tube with filter, to fully soak in the preservation solution. Repeat after 00:02:00 - 00:03:00 to re-soak. Filter ready for storage.

steps.title: When in the lab, add 20 µL, protK
steps.instruction: When in the lab, add 20 µL, protK .

steps.title: Follow standard protocol for blood samples
steps.instruction: Follow standard protocol for blood samples: Since using 400 µL volume, double amount of AL and Ethanol: add 400 µL, Buffer AL . Mix thoroughly by vortexing, add 400 µL, Ethanol . mix by vortexing, load on spin column, etc.. Pipetting is a bit of hassle with the filter in the tube, best to remove most buffer, spin shortly, remove rest. Also you will need to load the DNA filter tube twice to process all liquid. Elute in either recommended 200 µL, AE or use lower elution volume (down to 50 µL ) if more concentrated DNA is required.

steps.title: Add 400 µL, Buffer AL
steps.instruction: Add 400 µL, Buffer AL .

steps.title: Mix thoroughly by vortexing
steps.instruction: Mix thoroughly by vortexing.

steps.title: Add 400 µL, Ethanol
steps.instruction: Add 400 µL, Ethanol .

steps.title: Mix by vortexing
steps.instruction: Mix by vortexing.

steps.title: Load on spin column
steps.instruction: Load on spin column.

steps.title: Incubate at 56 °C for 00:10:00 - 00:30:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at 56 °C for 00:10:00 - 00:30:00 .

steps.title: Add 1 volume of genomic extraction...
steps.instruction: Add 1 volume of genomic extraction buffer, follow protocol (mix by vortexing, load on spin column, etc.). Pipetting is a bit of hassle with the filter in the tube, best to remove most buffer, spin shortly, remove rest. Elute in either recommended volume ( 50 µL ) or use higher elution volume if more dilute DNA is required.

steps.title: Use classic phenol/chlorophorm extraction, that works also very well
steps.instruction: Use classic phenol/chlorophorm extraction, that works also very well. I did this with the samples I preserved in Longmire solution, can also be done with samples in DNA/RNA shield. Before doing DNA extraction incubate for 00:30:00 with protK at 56 °C .

steps.title: With the larger filter units (500ml)...
steps.instruction: With the larger filter units (500ml) you also get larger filters, which do not fit in a 2ml tube. Either use a 15ml tube, or the following idea will also work: cut the filter from the 500 ml units in 4 equal pieces and process these separately. Isolate DNA from 2 quarters, store the other 2 in Longmire for later if something goes wrong with the isolation.

steps.title: If filters in 2ml tubes and...
steps.instruction: If filters in 2ml tubes and volume <1ml, extraction can be done in the 2ml tubes, much easier. If filter preserved in more than 1 ml liquid, transfer to 15ml falcon tubes, or use the 15ml tubes with the filters. Optional when using 15ml tubes: Add several sterile, DNA free, 4mm glass-beads.

steps.title: Add 30 µL, proteaseK
steps.instruction: Add 30 µL, proteaseK .

steps.title: Incubate for 00:30:00 – 02:00:00 at...
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:30:00 – 02:00:00 at 56 °C - 60 °C , with regular vortexing.

steps.title: Add 1 volume of Phenol/Chlorphorm/Isoamylalcohol (PCI)...
steps.instruction: Add 1 volume of Phenol/Chlorphorm/Isoamylalcohol (PCI) ( 2 mL - 3 mL usually).

steps.title: Homogenize on vortex mixer or vortex for ~ 00:02:00
steps.instruction: Homogenize on vortex mixer or vortex for ~ 00:02:00 .

steps.title: Spin down 00:10:00 at
steps.instruction: Spin down 00:10:00 at .

steps.title: Waterphase now pink or more dark red (some samples)
steps.instruction: Waterphase now pink or more dark red (some samples). PCI yellowish, but not pink. Collect waterphase.

steps.title: Second extraction with 2 mL - 3 mL, Chlorophorm/IAA
steps.instruction: Second extraction with 2 mL - 3 mL, Chlorophorm/IAA . Spin for 00:10:00 at .

steps.title: Collect ~ 2 mL - 3 mL of the waterphase
steps.instruction: Collect ~ 2 mL - 3 mL of the waterphase.

steps.title: Add 500 µL, NaAcetate 3M
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL, NaAcetate 3M .

steps.title: Invert some more for about 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Invert some more for about 00:05:00 . Often a red-pinkish colour develops (probably some reaction between the algal chlorophyll and the PCI?)

steps.title: Add 7 mL, cold EtOH
steps.instruction: Add 7 mL, cold EtOH . White precipitate forms, this is normal.

steps.title: Spin down for 00:15:00 at
steps.instruction: Spin down for 00:15:00 at . Sometimes yellowish sticky/slimy pellet visible(polysaccharides?), can be removed by washing with 70% EtOH. Necessary to resuspend the gooey pellet by shaking/vortexing.

steps.title: Wash pellet in 3 mL, 70% EtOH
steps.instruction: Wash pellet in 3 mL, 70% EtOH . (1/2)

steps.title: Wash pellet in 3 mL, 70% EtOH
steps.instruction: Wash pellet in 3 mL, 70% EtOH . (2/2)

steps.title: Dry pellet to air
steps.instruction: Dry pellet to air.

steps.title: Resuspend in 100 µL - 200 µL, NFW/T10E1/EB
steps.instruction: Resuspend in 100 µL - 200 µL, NFW/T10E1/EB .
